nodes.label: AI as time-saving tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as time-consuming or inefficient tool
nodes.grounding_span: heavy admin tasks that would otherwise take hours of my day

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for human thinking
nodes.grounding_span: less of a replacement for my brain and more of an extension to it

nodes.label: AI reliability for practical tasks
nodes.grounding_span: not very consistent with making a call on directly grading a student's work

nodes.label: AI as tool for lesson planning
nodes.label_negative: AI as source of polished but flawed output requiring more time to fix
nodes.grounding_span: going from hours spent on just a draft to a final version in about the same time is amazing

nodes.label: Human judgment in student assessment
nodes.grounding_span: that judgement is difficult to make, it's not always a black and white decision

nodes.label: Time for meaningful teaching
nodes.grounding_span: free me up even more to focus on supporting my students

nodes.label: Professional autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: I still plan lessons and map criteria myself

nodes.label: Quality and accuracy in teaching materials
nodes.grounding_span: make sure everything that I do aligns with the learning aims specified

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: Using AI to make full schemes of work always feels like a win

nodes.label: Frustration with AI training difficulty
nodes.grounding_span: The process and the right approach has definitely taken me a few weeks to perfect, which felt frustrating at first

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's inability to reconsider grading
nodes.grounding_span: I wouldn't hand over to AI, things like marking work

nodes.label: Cautious optimism about future integration
nodes.grounding_span: I see it becoming more seamless, less of a separate tool

nodes.label: Collaborative iterative refinement — works back and forth with AI
nodes.grounding_span: start with a clear structure in mind, then use AI to draft sections ... refine and adjust as I go along

nodes.label: Domain-based boundary setting — clear separation of AI-appropriate vs. human-only tasks
nodes.grounding_span: There's definitely parts that I wouldn't hand over to AI, things like marking work
